Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery And To Compel Further Production (And For Contempt) And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Daniel Ray Bacon, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lloyd Kraal
Sejal Ojha, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: The San Francisco Zoological Society

AFTER HEARING, Plaintiff Lloyd Kraal's Motion To Reopen Discovery And To Compel Further Production And For Contempt And For Sanctions is DENIED as there has not been a showing of good cause and, based upon this motion, the court cannot determine what additional discovery is required. The motion is DENIED as to Numbers 54,55,60, and 61 as they are protected by the right to privacy and are overly broad. Verifications have been provided. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-28-2009 Text Ruling

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2009 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Production Of Documents And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Daniel Ray Bacon, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Lloyd Kraal
Sejal Ojiha, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: S.F. Zoo

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Lloyd Kraal's Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents is granted as all objections except those based upon privilege are overruled and ordered stricken. Production of documents shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log within 15 days of notice of this order.

The court finds that the responses and late production were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,640, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
